summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering two zoning cases, the 2025 budget adoption, and a personnel matter. The Board approved a PUD zoning amendment for a 107-lot expansion of Lakefield Place subdivision with conditions requiring an emergency secondary access road. A major modification for HealthSource's building expansion from 7,700 to 16,234 square feet was approved with conditions including sidewalks and landscaping improvements. The 2025 budget was adopted after department heads made cuts totaling over $200,000, with a fire department station vehicle added back in at $50,000. The Board also accepted the resignation of Officer Chris Davidson.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (Nov 15-19, 2024) – passed unanimously

ZC-2024-006: Motion to approve Lakefield Place PUD expansion (R1 to PUD, 107 lots) with conditions requiring emergency secondary access road and revised preliminary plan showing PUD zoning on entire parcel 112209H018 – passed unanimously

ZC-2024-007: Motion to approve HealthSource major modification with conditions including sidewalks along Charles Snyder Road north to SR 28 (stipulated by ODOT), sidewalk connecting Shade Drive to Woodland Stream, lighting fixture height of 27.6 feet approved, landscaping screening of parking from SR 28, and removal of existing pavement between parking and SR 28 – passed unanimously
Motion to approve 2025 budget as presented with inclusion of $50,000 for fire department station vehicle – passed unanimously
Motion to accept resignation of Officer Chris Davidson effective November 30, 2024 – passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 7:37 p.m. – passed unanimously

attendees: James Broussard (Director of Facilities, Health Resources of Ohio/HealthSource)
notes: Reference made to a tragedy in Bethel earlier that day during the opening prayer. The meeting included a Township Budgeting 101 educational presentation by Administrator Rose aimed at public viewers. The township is in the top 4% of Ohio townships by population (15,000+) but operates without home rule. The police continuing levy passed in 2020 is at its halfway mark of an 8-10 year projected lifespan. HealthSource has been awarded Appalachia grant capital funding from the state of Ohio. ODOT approval still pending for SR 132 road improvements. It was Alyssa's birthday. The Clermont County Township Association meeting/Thanksgiving dinner scheduled for Thursday in Williamsburg.
title: BOT 11-19-24
